_header.author: R. A. Carucci
_header.purpose: Clery Act geographic categories for crime reporting - DOE Handbook for Campus Safety and Security Reporting

metadata.source: U.S. Dept. of Education, Office of Postsecondary Education, Handbook 2016

ON_CAMPUS.name: On-Campus
definition: Any building or property owned or controlled by an institution within the same reasonably contiguous geographic area and used by the institution in direct support of, or in a manner related to, the institution's educational purposes, including residence halls; and

Any building or property that is within or reasonably contiguous to the above, that is owned by the institution but controlled by another person, is frequently used by students, and supports institutional purposes (e.g., food or retail vendor).
includes: Campus-owned retail/dining (even if operated by third party)
excludes: Off-campus student housing (unless owned/controlled by institution)

NONCAMPUS.name: Noncampus
NONCAMPUS.plain_language: Properties not contiguous to core campus but used for educational purposes
definition: Any building or property owned or controlled by a student organization that is officially recognized by the institution; or

Any building or property owned or controlled by an institution that is used in direct support of, or in relation to, the institution's educational purposes, is frequently used by students, and is NOT within the same reasonably contiguous geographic area of the institution.
includes: Recognized student organization property (e.g., fraternity house)
NONCAMPUS.clarification: Noncampus does NOT mean 'off campus' - it refers to specific institution-owned or student-org-controlled properties.

PUBLIC_PROPERTY.name: Public Property
PUBLIC_PROPERTY.plain_language: Public property immediately adjacent to, within, or surrounding on-campus geography
PUBLIC_PROPERTY.definition: Public property immediately adjacent to, within, or surrounding one's on-campus geography.
includes: Public sidewalk on opposite side of street
Public property within core campus (e.g., public roads through campus)
excludes: Public transit stops beyond immediate campus border

reporting_notes.geography_required: Every Clery crime must be assigned to one of the three geographic categories.
reporting_notes.institution_responsibility: Institution must define and document its Clery geography annually.
reporting_notes.multi_campus: Institutions with multiple campuses report separately for each campus or provide aggregate as required by DOE.
